# Best Treatments for Melasma Near Houston, TX

Melasma is a common skin condition, and Houston has several excellent dermatology clinics offering a range of effective treatments. Here's a comprehensive overview:

---

## 🔬 What Is Melasma?

citations.cited_text:
All have been shown to be highly effective ...

Melasma is a skin condition that is characterized by irregular patches of pigmentation on the face. Th...
citations.title: Melasma Treatment Houston TX

content.text: Melasma is a skin condition characterized by irregular patches of pigmentation on the face. The tan or brown patches often occur symmetrically on the cheeks, upper lip, forehead, or nose.

citations.cited_text: The deeper the melasma or pigment is in the skin, the harder it is to treat. For instance, epidermal melasma is more superficial, generally appears we...
citations.title: Melasma Treatment Houston, TX | Treating Dark Spots & Hyperpigmentation

content.text: The deeper the melasma is in the skin, the harder it is to treat. Epidermal melasma is more superficial, generally appears well-defined and dark brown, and responds relatively well to treatment compared to deeper dermal melasma, which appears light brown or bluish and does not have clear borders.

---

## 💊 Treatment Options Available in Houston

### 1. **Topical Treatments (First-Line)**

citations.cited_text: The most commonly used treatments for melasma are skin lightening medications that are applied topically. These include medications such as hydroquino...
citations.title: Melasma: What are the best treatments? - Harvard Health

content.text: The most commonly used treatments for melasma are skin-lightening medications applied topically, including hydroquinone, azelaic acid, kojic acid, niacinamide, cysteamine, rucinol, and tranexamic acid.

citations.cited_text: Topical treatments are a key component in a well-rounded treatment regimen for melasma. Hydroquinone, tretinoin, and corticosteroids can be used alone...
citations.title: Best Melasma Treatment | Best Hyperpigmentation Treatment | Houston, Pearland, Webster, TX

content.text: Topical treatments are a key component in a well-rounded treatment regimen for melasma. Hydroquinone, tretinoin, and corticosteroids can be used alone or in combination to help with skin lightening.
### 2. **Chemical Peels**

citations.cited_text: Chemical peel: In this procedure, your dermatologist will put a chemical on your skin that may make it peel. The skin that regenerates should be smoot...
citations.title: Melasma: Treatment, Causes & Prevention

content.text: In this procedure, a dermatologist applies a chemical to the skin that may cause it to peel. The skin that regenerates should be smoother and more evenly colored.

citations.cited_text: If you are ready to feel great about your skin, Houston Skin can help create a successful treatment plan that includes one or more of the following: A...
citations.title: Best Melasma Treatment | Best Hyperpigmentation Treatment | Houston, Pearland, Webster, TX

content.text: A medical-grade chemical peel is one of the most popular and effective skin treatments to improve pigment issues.
### 3. **Laser & Light Therapy**

citations.cited_text: ... Laser therapy is an effective treatment option for addressing hyperpigmentation associated with melasma. Dr. Brickman uses the most advanced laser...
citations.title: Melasma Treatment in Houston TX - Symptoms & Causes - Houston Laser Skin Center

content.text: Laser therapy is an effective treatment option for addressing hyperpigmentation associated with melasma. Advanced laser technology can specifically target brown spots, and laser melasma treatments are quick, comfortable sessions that will be effective regardless of the size of the spots.
### 4. **Microneedling**

citations.cited_text: In some cases, oral therapy with tranexamic acid can be used. Undergoing in-office microneedling or chemical peel treatments at Elite Dermatology can ...
citations.title: Melasma in Houston, TX | Elite Dermatology & Plastic Surgery

content.text: In some cases, oral therapy with tranexamic acid can be used. Undergoing in-office microneedling or chemical peel treatments can also help even out your skin tone.
### 5. **Intense Pulsed Light (IPL) Photofacials**

citations.cited_text: To lighten the dark patches of melasma (no matter the cause), we offer topical treatments (hydroquinone or retinoid creams, gels, or lotions), medical...
citations.title: Melasma in Houston, TX | Advanced Dermatology

content.text: To lighten the dark patches of melasma, treatments include topical treatments (hydroquinone or retinoid creams, gels, or lotions), medical-grade chemical peels, dermabrasion, microneedling, intense pulsed light photofacials, and laser skin resurfacing.
### 6. **Oral Tranexamic Acid**

citations.cited_text: Tranexamic acid was the first choice for oral treatment (23.4%).

content.text: Tranexamic acid is the first choice for oral treatment
among many dermatologists.

citations.cited_text: In a single-center RCT comparing oral TXA with topical TXA in 50 patients with melasma, both were found to be effective and to have a low rate of side...

content.text: Both oral and topical tranexamic acid have been found to be effective with a low rate of side effects, including nasal and sinus discomfort, musculoskeletal and back pain, abdominal pain, and oligomenorrhea.

## ☀️ Key Tips for Managing Melasma

-

citations.cited_text: The key point in management of melasma is to use sun protection all the time, and to avoid other triggers such as hormonal medications when possible. ...
citations.title: Melasma: What are the best treatments? - Harvard Health

content.text: The key point in management of melasma is to use sun protection all the time and to avoid other triggers such as hormonal medications when possible. Since none of the available treatments are a cure, prevention is the best option.

citations.cited_text: Though effective, melasma treatments take time, and it may be a few months until you see improvements in your skin tone. Your ALLUSTRA DERMATOLOGY pro...
citations.title: Melasma for Houston, TX | Allustra Dermatology | Cosmetic, Medical, & Surgical Dermatologists

content.text: Melasma treatments take time, and it may be a few months until you see improvements in your skin tone. Your provider will likely recommend using sunscreen daily to protect your skin and prevent a recurrence.

citations.cited_text: Melasma needs a specialized diagnosis to ensure we prescribe the best treatment for your skin. If the incorrect remedy is applied, the skin may become...
citations.title: Melasma in Houston, TX | Dr. Papasakelariou

content.text: Melasma needs a specialized diagnosis to ensure the best treatment for your skin. If the incorrect remedy is applied, the skin may become darker.

---

**Bottom Line:** The best treatment depends on the depth and severity of your melasma. A combination approach — topical agents + sun protection + an in-office procedure like a chemical peel or laser — tends to yield the best results. It's strongly recommended to schedule a consultation with a **board-certified dermatologist** in the Houston area for a personalized treatment plan.
